claims.claim: Circuit Tracks has exactly two synth tracks, Synth 1 (violet pads) and Synth 2 (pale green pads), which operate identically apart from colour.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p27 'The Synths / Playing a Synth'

claims.claim: Each synth track is six-note polyphonic: up to six notes can be assigned to any single step if the patch is polyphonic.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p35 'Recording a synth pattern in real time'

claims.claim: The synth engines are the Nova-family engines (dual-oscillator wavetable/virtual-analogue polysynth), the same engine family as the original Circuit.
sources: Sound On Sound review, https://www.soundonsound.com/reviews/novation-circuit-tracks
DJTechTools review, https://djtechtools.com/2021/05/27/review-novations-circuit-tracks-is-an-even-better-circuit-groovebox/

claims.claim: Each synth has 128 factory patches, arranged as four pages of 32, opened by pressing the Preset button (Patch View).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p33 'Selecting Patches'

claims.claim: Pressing Preset with Synth 1 selected opens Patch View Page 1; with Synth 2 selected it opens Page 3; pages are navigated with the down/up arrow buttons.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p33

claims.claim: Default patch is Patch 1 (Page 1 Pad 1) for Synth 1 and Patch 33 (Page 2 Pad 1) for Synth 2.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p33

claims.claim: Patches audition on selection (Patch Preview), respecting current scale/root; holding Shift while pressing a patch pad disables preview.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p33-34

claims.claim: Patches can be recalled externally via MIDI Program Change: default Channel 1 for Synth 1, Channel 2 for Synth 2.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p34 'External Patch Select'

claims.claim: There are eight endless-rotary macro knobs; each macro can tweak up to four patch parameters at once.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p34 'Exploring the Macros'

claims.claim: The eight macros' default panel labels are: 1 Oscillator, 2 Oscillator Mod, 3 Amp Envelope, 4 Filter Envelope, 5 Filter Frequency, 6 Resonance, 7 Modulation, 8 FX; these are only the primary function and patches can reassign them.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, cover panel image and p34

claims.claim: Deep synth editing (assigning any four parameters per knob with range/depth, building patches from scratch) is done in Novation Components' Synth Editor, not on the hardware; on-device sound design is limited to the eight macros.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p34 and Introduction p5
Sound On Sound review

claims.claim: Up to 16 musical scales are available, set in Scales View by pressing the Scales button; only Chromatic contains more than eight notes (12).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p30-31 'Scales'

claims.claim: The 16 scales on pads 17-32 are: Natural Minor, Major, Dorian, Phrygian, Mixolydian, Melodic Minor (ascending), Harmonic Minor, Bebop Dorian, Blues, Minor Pentatonic, Hungarian Minor, Ukranian Dorian, Marva, Todi, Whole Tone, Chromatic.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p31 scale table

claims.claim: The default root note is C (keynote Pad 9, lit darker blue); pressing a different keynote pad in Scales View transposes the keyboard, and keynote pads always show an octave from C to B.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p32 'Root note'

claims.claim: Changing scale or root note is non-destructive and can be done while a project is playing; notes absent from a new scale are intelligently remapped (usually a semitone) and revert when the original scale is restored.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p32-33

claims.claim: In Note View the two lower rows are the synth keyboard and the upper row is one octave higher; the synth has a 10-octave total range accessed in octave pairs via the down/up arrows; Chromatic scale yields only a one-octave keyboard.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p28, p31

claims.claim: Expanded Note View (Shift + Note; pads turn gold) removes the step display and adds the next two higher octaves for a four-octave playing surface.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p28-29 'Expanded Note View'

claims.claim: There are four independent drum tracks (Drum 1-4), each a discrete sequencer track, and each can use any one of 64 pre-loaded samples.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p60, p62 'Selecting Samples'

claims.claim: Drum samples appear as four pages of 16 in Note View, or two pages of 32 in Preset View (opened by pressing the track button then Preset).
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p60, p62

claims.claim: Default sample allocation: Drum 1 = Kick 1 (Page1 slot1), Drum 2 = Snare 1 (slot3), Drum 3 = Closed hi-hat 1 (slot5), Drum 4 = Additional percussion 1 (slot9); each page is a kit with slots 1-2 kicks, 3-4 snares, 5-6 closed hats, 7-8 open hats, 9-12 percussion, 13-16 melodic.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p60

claims.claim: A quick tap on a drum sample pad changes the track's active sample; a long press plays the sample without reassigning the active sample.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p61

claims.claim: Sample Flip assigns different samples per step: press-and-hold a sample pad (turns red) then press step pads (turn red); flipped steps light pink in the pattern display versus bright blue for normal hits, and it can be done independently per drum track.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p62-63 'Sample Flip'

claims.claim: On drum tracks only the even macros are active: Macro 2 = Pitch, Macro 4 = Decay envelope time, Macro 6 = Distortion, Macro 8 = EQ.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p63 'Using the Macros for drum design'

claims.claim: Expanded Drum View (Shift + Note) turns pads 29-32 into live triggers for Drums 1-4 simultaneously, disabling all other pads.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p61 'Expanded Drum View'

claims.claim: The FX section provides 16 delay presets and 8 reverb presets; only one delay and one reverb preset can be active globally, but send amounts are set independently per track with the macros.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p90-92 'FX Section'

claims.claim: In FX View delay presets are on the peach pads of Rows 1-2 (pads 1-16, increasing complexity), reverb presets on the cream pads of Row 3 (pads 17-24, increasing reverb time); all delays are BPM-synced.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p90

claims.claim: The 8 reverb presets are: Small Chamber, Small Room 1, Small Room 2, Large Room, Hall, Large Hall, Hall long reflection, Large Hall long reflection.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p91 reverb table

claims.claim: The 16 delay presets include Slapback Fast/Slow, 32nd Triplets, 32nd, 16th Triplets, 16th, 16th Ping Pong, 16th Ping Pong Swung, 8th Triplets, 8th dotted Ping Pong, 8th, 8th Ping Pong, 8th Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets, 4th dotted Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets Ping Pong Wide.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p92 delay table

claims.claim: A master compressor is applied to the whole mix by default and is not adjustable; it is only toggled on/off via the FX button in Setup/Advanced Setup View.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p92 'Master Compressor', p106

claims.claim: Side Chain View is opened with Shift + FX; it offers seven side-chain presets per source and is OFF by default on both synths and both MIDI tracks.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p93 'Side Chains'

claims.claim: Side-chain lets the hits of a chosen drum track duck the audio level of a synth track or external audio input; Page 1 covers Synth 1/2, Page 2 covers the external inputs (MIDI 1/2); Pad 1 in each row is OFF (red), pads 2-8 are the 7 presets, and top-row pads 5-8 select which drum (1-4) is the trigger.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p93-94

claims.claim: Side-chain ducking continues even if the trigger drum's Mixer level is zero, but muting that drum in Mixer View disables side-chain triggering.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p94

claims.claim: The Master Filter knob filters the entire output (all six internal tracks plus both external inputs); it has a centre detent (no filtering, LED dim white), anticlockwise = low-pass, clockwise = high-pass, LED pale blue when active.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p94 'The Filter Knob'

claims.claim: Mixer View (press Mixer) provides an eight-channel mixer with default track level 100 (range 0-127); Row 1 pads are per-track Mute buttons; there is no Solo function.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p88 'Mixer'

claims.claim: In Mixer View the macros default to level control (macros 3 and 4 controlling external Inputs 1 and 2); pressing the down arrow switches the macros to pan control (centre = white LED, left = blue, right = pink), and both level and pan are automatable.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p88-89 'Input Level control' and 'Panning'

claims.claim: Main audio outputs are two 1/4-inch TS jacks (L/Mono and R); with no plug in R, L/Mono carries a mono sum; max output +5.3 dBu. Inputs 1 and 2 are two 1/4-inch TS unbalanced line-level inputs.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p18 'Rear view'

claims.claim: The USB-C port carries power and MIDI (class-compliant) but does NOT carry audio; recording into a DAW must use the analogue outputs.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p18 'Rear view'

claims.claim: The headphone output stays active alongside the main outputs when plugged in, and drives +5 dBu into 150-ohm headphones; a 3.5mm Sync out supplies a 5V analogue clock at a default 2 pulses per quarter note.
sources: Circuit Tracks User Guide v3, p18 'Rear view'

verdicts.note: User Guide p22 color table + p27/p33: two synth tracks, Synth 1 pads violet, Synth 2 pale green; 'The Note Views for the four drum tracks are the same, apart from the colour coding' logic mirrors synths. Functionally identical apart from colour (they do differ in default patch/page and default MIDI channel, but operation is the same). Confirmed.

User Guide p35: "Circuit Tracks' synth engines are 'six-note polyphonic' - that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern, if the Patch you've selected is suitably polyphonic." Feature list p6 also states 'Two synth tracks with 6-voice polyphony.'

Not in the official manual (which never names the engine), but confirmed by Sound On Sound review: the Tracks has 'two on-board synths (using the same Nova engines)' as the original Circuit, described as 'dual-oscillator wavetable/virtual analogue polysynths.' Same Nova engine family as the 2015 Circuit.
User Guide p33 'Selecting Patches': 'Each of the two synth tracks can use any one of 128 pre-loaded Patches... arranged as four pages of 32 in Patch View,' opened by pressing Preset.

User Guide p33: pressing Preset 'opens Patch View Page 1 if Synth 1 is selected and Patch View Page 3 if Synth 2 is selected. In each case, you can select the other pages by using the down/up arrow buttons.' (The 'J/K' glyphs in the PDF are the down/up arrows, confirmed via Novation online guide.)

User Guide p33: 'Patch 1 (Pad 1 on Page 1) is the default Patch for Synth 1 and Patch 33 (Pad 1 on Page 2) is the default Patch for Synth 2.' Note Synth 2's default patch (33, page 2) differs from the page it opens on (page 3) - both are stated verbatim.

User Guide p33-34 'Patch Preview': 'Patch Preview takes into account the current Scale and Root Note setting when auditioning a Patch. If you hold down Shift while pressing a pad in Patch View, Preview is disabled.'

User Guide p34 'External Patch Select': recalled via MIDI Program Change, 'the default MIDI channels are Channel 1 (Synth 1) and Channel 2 (Synth 2), though MIDI channels may be reassigned in Setup View.'

User Guide p34 'Exploring the Macros': eight Macro controls; 'Each of the 128 factory Patches... can have up to four of its parameters tweaked by each Macro control.' 'The rotary controls are endless.'

Confirmed by the Mixer/panel labels in the guide: 1 Oscillator, 2 Oscillator Mod, 3 Amp Envelope, 4 Filter Envelope, 5 Filter Frequency, 6 Resonance, 7 Modulation, 8 FX. Guide p34: 'The primary function of each Macro is indicated below the knob' and 'With certain Patches, some Macros will be assigned a function quite different to their normal one.'

User Guide p34 notes patches 'created using Novation Components' Synth Editor' and p33 'You can use Components to load Patches.' On-device sound shaping is limited to the eight macros; deep parameter assignment/patch building is done in Components. Sound On Sound review concurs.

User Guide p31 'Scales': 'Up to 16 musical scales are available... Not all these scales contain eight notes, though the only one that has more than eight is Chromatic, with 12.' Set in Scales View via the Scales button.

User Guide p31 scale table (pads 17-32) exactly: Natural Minor, Major, Dorian, Phrygian, Mixolydian, Melodic Minor (ascending), Harmonic Minor, Bebop Dorian, Blues, Minor Pentatonic, Hungarian Minor, Ukranian Dorian, Marva, Todi, Whole Tone, Chromatic. Order matches the claim verbatim.

User Guide p32 'Root note': 'The default root note for all scales is C... Pad 9, corresponding to C, is lit a darker blue than the other pads.' 'the keynote selection pads always show an octave from C to B.' Pressing another keynote pad transposes.

User Guide p32-33: changing keynote transposes 'even while the Project is playing'; changing scale, missing notes get 'an intelligent decision as to which note to play instead, which will normally be either one semitone above or below the original note. Changing scale in this way is not destructive: you can revert to the original scale.'

User Guide p28: two lower grid rows are the synth keyboard (play area), 'the upper row of the synth keyboard contains notes one octave above those in the second row' (highest note of lower octave = lowest note of higher octave). p28: 'The synth has a total range of 10 octaves; you can access higher or lower pairs of octaves' via the arrows. Chromatic reduces the keyboard to one octave (p31/p32).

User Guide p29 'Expanded Note View': 'hold down Shift and press Note; Note now illuminates gold... removes the pattern display in the two upper rows... replacing it with keys for the next two higher octaves of the selected scale' - i.e., a four-octave surface.
User Guide p60-63: 'Circuit Tracks has four separate drum tracks, Drum 1 to Drum 4,' each programmed independently; 'Each of the four drum tracks... can use any one of 64 pre-loaded samples.'

User Guide p63 'Selecting Samples': audition/select 'in Note View, four pages of 16 at a time, or use Preset View... the samples are arranged as two pages of 32.' Preset View opened by pressing the track button then Preset.

User Guide p60: 'default sample page allocation: Drum 1: Page 1, slot 1 (Kick 1); Drum 2: Page 1, slot 3 (Snare 1); Drum 3: Page 1, slot 5 (Closed hi hat 1); Drum 4: Page 1, slot 9 (Additional percussion 1).' Kit layout: 'Drums 1 and 2 are kick drums, 3 and 4 are snares, 5 and 6 are closed hi hats, 7 and 8 are open hi hats, 9 through 12... additional percussion, and 13 through 16 are melodic sounds.'
User Guide p61: 'To change the active sample, give a different sample pad a quick tap: a longer press will play the sample but leave the previous sample assigned as the active one.'

User Guide p62-63 'Sample Flip': 'Press and hold the pad for the required sample (it turns red) and then press the step pads... they also turn red.' 'steps with hits of the active sample illuminate bright blue, but any that have been flipped illuminate pink.' 'You can do this independently for each of the four drums.'

User Guide p63/p64 'Using the Macros for drum design': 'Only the even-numbered Macros are active when a drum track is selected.' Table: Macro 2 = Pitch, 4 = Decay envelope time, 6 = Distortion, 8 = EQ.

User Guide p61-62 'Expanded Drum View': opened by 'holding Shift and pressing Note'; 'all the grid pads except 29-32 are disabled'; pads 29-32 are 'Playing Pads for Drums 1-4' triggering each track's current active sample simultaneously.

User Guide p91-92: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided, and you can select any one of each type.' 'It is not possible to use different reverb presets on different tracks' (one delay + one reverb globally). Send amounts are per-track via the 8 macros (same layout as Mixer).

User Guide p91: 'Each of the peach pads on Rows 1 and 2 calls up a delay preset, and similarly, the cream pads on Row 3 enable reverb presets... reverb presets are arranged with increasing reverberation time from Pad 17 to Pad 24, and the delay presets with increasing complexity from Pad 1 to Pad 16... In all cases, delay time is related to the BPM.'
User Guide p91 reverb table matches exactly: 1 Small Chamber, 2 Small Room 1, 3 Small Room 2, 4 Large Room, 5 Hall, 6 Large Hall, 7 Hall - long reflection, 8 Large Hall - long reflection.

User Guide p92 delay table matches all 16: Slapback Fast, Slapback Slow, 32nd Triplets, 32nd, 16th Triplets, 16th, 16th Ping Pong, 16th Ping Pong Swung, 8th Triplets, 8th dotted Ping Pong, 8th, 8th Ping Pong, 8th Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets, 4th dotted Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets Ping Pong Wide.

User Guide p90/p93: 'There is also a master compressor which is applied to your mix by default.' 'Master Compressor - This is enabled or disabled by the FX button in Setup View: see page 103.' Only on/off control exists (no adjustable parameters). Minor: manual cites Setup View p103; the claim's 'Setup/Advanced Setup View' is substantively correct.

User Guide p94 'Side Chains': 'Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button. Open by holding Shift and pressing FX.' 'Seven Side Chain presets are available.' 'The default state is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks.'

User Guide p94: Page 1 = Synth 1/Synth 2, Page 2 = MIDI (Audio In) 1/2. 'seven Side Chain Presets (Pads 2 to 8 in each row); the first pad in each row is the OFF button... Pad 1 is illuminated bright red when the Side Chain is OFF.' 'Pads 5 to 8 on the top row let you select which Drum track will be the side chain trigger.' Ducks synth or external audio input by the selected drum's hits.

User Guide p94: 'Side Chain ducking will continue even when the level of the source (trigger drum) track is reduced to zero in the Mixer View... However, if you mute the drum track selected as the key in Mixer View, side chain triggering is disabled.'

User Guide p95 'The Filter Knob': filters 'the sum of the sounds from all six internal tracks plus the two external audio inputs.' 'controls a low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre position and a high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise.' Centre detent = no filtering, LED dimly lit white; LED changes to pale blue when active.

User Guide p89 'Mixer': 'eight-channel mixer... all the tracks play at a volume level of 100 (arbitrary units, range 0-127).' 'The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track.' No Solo function exists on Circuit Tracks (only Mute is documented).

User Guide p89: macros default to level control (down-arrow lit); Macros 3 and 4 control external Inputs 1 and 2. 'Pressing the [down arrow] button converts the Macro controls to pan controls... default pan position... stereo-centre, indicated by the Macro LEDs showing white. Panning a track left turns the LED... bright blue; panning to the right... bright pink.' Both level and pan automatable. (Verified the down-arrow=pan mapping via Novation's online Mixer article, since the PDF renders the arrow glyph as 'J'.)

User Guide p17-18 'Rear view': 'Outputs - L/Mono and R... on two 1/4" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' 'Inputs 1 and 2 - two external audio inputs: line level signals... unbalanced on 1/4" TS jack sockets.'

User Guide p18: USB-C port is 'the DC power input... The port is MIDI class compliant... NOTE - Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' Recording into a DAW therefore requires the analogue outputs (direct inference from 'does not carry audio').

User Guide p18: Headphones - 'The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' Sync - '3.5 mm TRS jack socket supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude... The default rate is two pulses per quarter note' (ratio configurable in Setup View).

verification.overallNotes:
All 38 sound-angle claims verified against the OFFICIAL Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide (v3 EN, official copy at fael-downloads-prod.focusrite.com/customer/prod/downloads/circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf; verified via an identical mirror). Page numbers cited by the claims match the actual guide. sound-3 is the only claim not covered by the manual (which never names the engine); it is confirmed by the Sound On Sound review ("same Nova engines," "dual-oscillator wavetable/virtual analogue polysynths"). Every claim is explicitly about Circuit Tracks, not the original Circuit or Circuit Rhythm.

One glyph caveat resolved: the PDF renders the down/up arrow buttons as "J" and "K" (font-encoding artifact). I confirmed via Novation's official online Mixer article that the DOWN arrow (down-triangle) is what switches macros to pan and the UP arrow returns to level - so sound-35's "down arrow" wording is correct. The same J/K = down/up arrows apply to sound-5's page navigation (correct).

IMPORTANT SOUND-ANGLE FACTS THE CLAIMS MISSED ENTIRELY:
1. Drum sample/patch recall via MIDI: drum samples can be recalled externally by MIDI CC messages on MIDI Channel 10 (User Guide p63) - parallel to the synth Program Change facility in sound-8, but omitted.
2. Factory default patch character: default Projects load with a MONOPHONIC sound for Synth 1 and a POLYPHONIC sound for Synth 2 (User Guide p35). Relevant context for the six-note polyphony claim (sound-2) - out of the box the two synths behave differently.
3. Drum macros are FIXED-function (unlike synth macros which patches can reassign) - the guide explicitly contrasts this (p64): "Unlike the synth Macros, the functions are fixed for drums."
4. FX send macros indicate mode by LED colour: reverb send macros light dim CREAM, delay send macros light PEACH, matching the pad colours (User Guide p91-92). Reverb/delay send levels are automatable in Record Mode (p92).
5. Synth-keyboard octave overlap: the highest note of the lower keyboard row (Pad 32) is the SAME pitch as the lowest note of the higher row (Pad 17) - an important playing-surface detail (User Guide p28).
6. Note View note-shift range: the arrows shift synth/MIDI pitch "up by one to five octaves, or down by one to six octaves," adjustable independently per track (User Guide p17) - the mechanism behind the 10-octave range in sound-16.
7. Sync-out ratio is user-configurable in Setup View (default 2 ppqn is only the default); the guide notes "the actual ratio can be set in Setup View" (p18).
8. Master Volume is a separate analogue output-level knob (control 4), distinct from the per-track mixer levels and the Master Filter (User Guide p18/p89).
9. Chromatic scale has a DIFFERENT keyboard layout (piano-style, matching the keynote-selection pads) rather than the standard two-row scale layout (User Guide p31-32) - noted for sound-16 but the layout specifics were not.
10. Side-chain terminology: the trigger drum is called the "key"; a good demo setup (synth note Gate 16 + Drum 1 kicks) is documented (p94) - minor, but the "key" wording matters for accuracy.

verdicts.note: User Guide v3 p27: Synth 1 keys illuminated violet, Synth 2 pale green; behaviour is identical apart from colour coding. Minor nuance: factory-default Projects load a monophonic patch on Synth 1 and a polyphonic patch on Synth 2 (default patch differs), but the tracks are functionally identical. Circuit Tracks-specific.

p35: 'Circuit Tracks' synth engines are six-note polyphonic - that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern, if the Patch you've selected is suitably polyphonic.' Also spec line: 'Two synth tracks with 6-voice polyphony.'

Sound On Sound review: 'two on-board synths (using the same Nova engines)' and 'dual-oscillator wavetable/virtual analogue polysynths.' Lineage confirmed: engine derived from MiniNova (2012), Nova-class family (Supernova->UltraNova->MiniNova->Circuit/Circuit Tracks); same engine as original Circuit. MusicRadar corroborates two oscillators with VA waves and wavetables.
p33: 'Each of the two synth tracks can use any one of 128 pre-loaded Patches... arranged as four pages of 32 in Patch View. To open Patch View... press Preset.' Circuit Tracks-specific.
p33: pressing Preset 'opens Patch View Page 1 if Synth 1 is selected and Patch View Page 3 if Synth 2 is selected... select the other pages by using the down/up (J/K) buttons.'
p33: 'Patch 1 (Pad 1 on Page 1) is the default Patch for Synth 1 and Patch 33 (Pad 1 on Page 2) is the default Patch for Synth 2.' Exact match.
p33-34: 'Patch Preview takes into account the current Scale and Root Note setting when auditioning a Patch. If you hold down Shift while pressing a pad in Patch View, Preview is disabled.'
p34 External Patch Select: 'the default MIDI channels are Channel 1 (Synth 1) and Channel 2 (Synth 2)' for MIDI Program Change recall; channels reassignable in Setup View.
p34: eight Macro controls, 'endless' rotary; 'Each of the 128 factory Patches... can have up to four of its parameters tweaked by each Macro control.'

Panel legend (reproduced in FX/drum diagrams): 1 Oscillator, 2 Oscillator Mod, 3 Amp Envelope, 4 Filter Envelope, 5 Filter Frequency, 6 Resonance, 7 Modulation, 8 FX. Guide notes these are the primary function and patches can reassign them ('some Macros will be assigned a function quite different to their normal one'). Exact match.

p34 references patches 'created using Novation Components' Synth Editor'; on-device the guide describes only the eight Macros for sound modification. Deep four-parameters-per-knob editing is a Components feature. Consistent with Sound On Sound.
p30-31: 'Scales View, accessed by pressing the Scales button'; 16 scales; Chromatic is the only scale with more than eight notes: 'all 12 notes in the scale are available.'

p31 scale table pads 17-32 exactly: Natural Minor, Major, Dorian, Phrygian, Mixolydian, Melodic Minor (ascending), Harmonic Minor, Bebop Dorian, Blues, Minor Pentatonic, Hungarian Minor, Ukranian Dorian, Marva, Todi, Whole Tone, Chromatic. Note guide spells it 'Ukranian' (sic).
p32: 'The default root note for all scales is C... Pad 9, corresponding to C, is lit a darker blue... the keynote selection pads always show an octave from C to B.'

p32-33: 'you can change the keynote to transpose the notes, even while the Project is playing... Circuit Tracks makes an intelligent decision... normally either one semitone above or below... Changing scale in this way is not destructive: you can revert to the original scale and the notes will sound as they did before.'

p28/p31: two lower rows are the keyboard, upper row one octave higher (Pad 32 = Pad 17); 'The synth has a total range of 10 octaves; you can access higher or lower pairs of octaves by using the down/up (J/K) buttons.' Chromatic yields a one-octave keyboard.

p28: 'hold down Shift and press Note; Note now illuminates gold... Expanded Note View... removes the pattern display... replacing it with keys for the next two higher octaves.' Correction: it is the NOTE BUTTON that illuminates gold, not the pads as the claim states; the four-octave surface, step-display removal, and Shift+Note trigger are all correct.
p59/p62: 'Circuit Tracks has four separate drum tracks, Drum 1 to Drum 4', each independently programmable; 'Each of the four drum tracks can use any one of 64 pre-loaded samples.'
p60/p62: 'four pages of these (each with 16 samples)' in Note View; in Preset View 'the samples are arranged as two pages of 32.' Preset View opened via track button then Preset.

p60 exactly: Drum 1 = Page1 slot1 (Kick 1), Drum 2 = slot3 (Snare 1), Drum 3 = slot5 (Closed hi hat 1), Drum 4 = slot9 (Additional percussion 1). Kit layout: 1-2 kicks, 3-4 snares, 5-6 closed hats, 7-8 open hats, 9-12 additional percussion, 13-16 melodic.
p61: 'To change the active sample, give a different sample pad a quick tap: a longer press will play the sample but leave the previous sample assigned as the active one.'

p62: 'Press and hold the pad for the required sample (it turns red) and then press the step pads... they also turn red... steps with hits of the active sample illuminate bright blue, but any that have been flipped illuminate pink.' Sample Flip can be done independently per drum track. Exact match.
p63: 'Only the even-numbered Macros are active when a drum track is selected'; table: Macro 2 = Pitch, 4 = Decay envelope time, 6 = Distortion, 8 = EQ. Exact match.
p61 Expanded Drum View: Shift+Note; 'all the grid pads except 29-32 are disabled' - pads 29-32 become live triggers for Drums 1-4 simultaneously.

p89-90: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided, and you can select any one of each type'; 'It is not possible to use different reverb presets on different tracks'; send levels individually adjustable per track via Macros.

p90: 'Each of the peach pads on Rows 1 and 2 calls up a delay preset... the cream pads on Row 3 enable reverb presets... reverb presets arranged with increasing reverberation time from Pad 17 to Pad 24, and the delay presets with increasing complexity from Pad 1 to Pad 16... delay time is related to the BPM.'
p91 reverb table exactly: Small Chamber, Small Room 1, Small Room 2, Large Room, Hall, Large Hall, Hall - long reflection, Large Hall - long reflection.

p92 delay table exactly (16): Slapback Fast, Slapback Slow, 32nd Triplets, 32nd, 16th Triplets, 16th, 16th Ping Pong, 16th Ping Pong Swung, 8th Triplets, 8th dotted Ping Pong, 8th, 8th Ping Pong, 8th Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets, 4th dotted Ping Pong Swung, 4th Triplets Ping Pong Wide.

p89/p92: 'There is also a master compressor which is applied to your mix by default'; 'This is enabled or disabled by the FX button in Setup View.' Minor: guide names it 'Setup View' (page 102); Advanced Setup View is a separate view for MIDI Thru clone etc. Substance (whole-mix default, not adjustable, FX-button toggle) confirmed.

p93: 'Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button. Open by holding Shift and pressing FX'; 'Seven Side Chain presets are available'; 'The default state is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks.'

p93-94: Page 1 = Synth 1/Synth 2, Page 2 = MIDI 1/MIDI 2 (external inputs). 'seven Side Chain Presets (Pads 2 to 8 in each row)... the first pad in each row is the OFF button... Pad 1 is illuminated bright red when the Side Chain is OFF... Pads 5 to 8 on the top row let you select which Drum track will be the side chain trigger.' Exact match.

p94: 'Side Chain ducking will continue even when the level of the source track is reduced to zero in the Mixer View... if you mute the drum track selected as the key in Mixer View, side chain triggering is disabled.'

p94: 'The entire audio output... the sum of the sounds from all six internal tracks plus the two external audio inputs... low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre... high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise... detent in the centre - in this position no filtering takes place and the LED... is dimly lit white... LED changes to pale blue when either filter type is active.'

p88: 'Circuit Tracks includes an eight-channel mixer... all the tracks play at a volume level of 100 (arbitrary units, range 0-127)... The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track.' No Solo function exists - only Mute is documented.

p88-89: macros default to level control (Macros 3 and 4 control external Inputs 1 and 2); pressing the arrow (down/J) converts Macros to pan (default centre = white LED, left = bright blue, right = bright pink). Both level and pan automatable in Record Mode. Note: guide labels the arrow buttons J/K; J lit = level, pressing switches to pan with K lit.

p18: 'main audio outputs on two 1/4" TS jack sockets. Max output level is +5.3 dBu... With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix.' Inputs 1 and 2: 'unbalanced on 1/4" TS jack sockets' line-level. Exact match.
p18: USB-C 'is also the DC power input... The port is MIDI class compliant... NOTE - Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' So DAW audio recording must use the analogue outputs.

p18: 'The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' Sync: '3.5 mm TRS jack socket supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude... default rate is two pulses per quarter note.'

verification.overallNotes:
Source of record: official Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide v3 (focusrite CDN PDF), fetched and text-extracted; corroborated by Sound On Sound and MusicRadar for engine lineage. All 38 claims are specifically true of the Circuit TRACKS — no cross-device contamination detected. Only correction: sound-17 says the PADS turn gold in Expanded Note View, but per p28 it is the NOTE BUTTON that illuminates gold (four-octave surface / step-display-removal / Shift+Note all correct).

Facts the claims missed or under-specified for the 'sound' angle:
1) Engine lineage detail (strengthens sound-3): the synth engine is derived from the Novation MiniNova (2012), a Nova-class engine tracing to the Supernova (1990s) -> UltraNova -> MiniNova -> Circuit/Circuit Tracks. Circuit Tracks exposes fewer controls than MiniNova.
2) Oscillator depth (Components Synth Editor): each of the two oscillators offers 30 waveforms = 14 virtual-analogue waves + 16 wavetables, with Index (pulse width / wavetable position) and Interpolate controls; the filter offers low/high/bandpass modes each with two slopes, plus resonance and a Q width parameter — all only editable in Components, not on the hardware macros (context for sound-11).
3) Factory default patch character: Synth 1 default patch is monophonic, Synth 2 default is polyphonic (nuance behind sound-1's 'operate identically').
4) The two 'MIDI tracks' double as the two external audio inputs throughout the mixer/FX/side-chain — Inputs 1 & 2 are governed by Macros 3 & 4 in Mixer View, can receive their own reverb/delay sends, and can be side-chain-ducked (relevant to sound-25, 31, 33).
5) Master compressor is toggled specifically via the FX button in Setup View (p102); it is otherwise non-adjustable (sound-29 loosely cited 'Setup/Advanced Setup View' — it is Setup View).
6) Pan/level automation, FX-send automation, and drum-macro moves are all recordable per-pattern (Record Mode), deletable via Clear + turning the macro (LED goes red) — relevant to how sound is shaped over time.
7) Sync Out ratio (default 2 ppqn) is user-configurable in Setup View; tempo range is 40-240 BPM internal, 30-300 BPM to external clock (context for sound-38).
8) Velocity is 7-bit (0-127); drum tracks have Velocity View and Gate View (with Micro Steps) rather than a visible step row, which is where per-hit sound/timing is edited.

verdicts.note: User Guide v3 p27 (The Synths / Playing a Synth): 'The two synth tracks – Synth 1 and Synth 2 - operate identically. The only difference is the pad colour – Synth 1 keys are illuminated violet and Synth 2 keys are pale green.' Minor nuance omitted by the claim: high/low notes in each octave are a paler shade than the intermediate keys, but the core claim is exact.

p35: 'Circuit Tracks' synth engines are six-note polyphonic – that is, you can assign up to six notes to any step in the pattern, if the Patch you've selected is suitably polyphonic.' Note (missed by claim): the factory default Projects load Synth 1 as MONOphonic and Synth 2 as polyphonic (p36 tip box), so six-note poly is patch-dependent.

Not a manual claim; verified via Novation Support ('Circuit and Circuit Tracks Synth Engine Explained') and MusicRadar: both Circuit and Circuit Tracks use the same Nova engine family, based on the Novation MiniNova, with dual oscillators, noise, ring mod, multimode filters, 3 envelopes, 2 LFOs, per-patch FX. 'Same engine family as the original Circuit' and 'dual-oscillator wavetable/virtual-analogue' are supported.
p33: 'Each of the two synth tracks can use any one of 128 pre-loaded Patches... arranged as four pages of 32 in Patch View. To open Patch View for the currently selected synth, press Preset.'
p33: 'This opens Patch View Page 1 if Synth 1 is selected and Patch View Page 3 if Synth 2 is selected. In each case, you can select the other pages by using the down and up buttons.' Exact.

p33: 'Patch 1 (Pad 1 on Page 1) is the default Patch for Synth 1 and Patch 33 (Pad 1 on Page 2) is the default Patch for Synth 2.' Exact. (Quirk in the manual, not the claim: Synth 2's default patch sits on Page 2 yet pressing Preset for Synth 2 opens Page 3 per sound-5 — both statements are verbatim from p33.)

p33: 'Patch Preview takes into account the current Scale and Root Note setting when auditioning a Patch.' p34: 'If you hold down Shift while pressing a pad in Patch View, Preview is disabled.' (Also disabled during active playback in Record Mode.)
p34 (External Patch Select): 'the default MIDI channels are Channel 1 (Synth 1) and Channel 2 (Synth 2)', via MIDI Program Change (PGM) messages; channels reassignable in Setup View.
p34: 'Each of the 128 factory Patches... can have up to four of its parameters tweaked by each Macro control' and 'The rotary controls are endless'; p36 confirms 'any of the eight Macro controls'.

Panel diagram on p63 lists the synth macro labels exactly: 1 Oscillator, 2 Oscillator Mod, 3 Amp Envelope, 4 Filter Envelope, 5 Filter Frequency, 6 Resonance, 7 Modulation, 8 FX. p34 confirms these are the 'primary function' and that patches (e.g. from Components) can reassign a macro to a different function.

p33: 'You can use Components to load Patches onto Circuit Tracks'; p34 tip references 'Novation Components' Synth Editor' for patches beyond factory ones. On-device sound design is via the eight macros only; deep parameter assignment/patch-building is done in Components. Supported.
p30: 'Up to 16 musical scales are available... the only one that has more than eight is Chromatic, with 12'; 'set using the Scales View, accessed by pressing the Scales button.'

p31 scale table (pads 17-32) matches the claim exactly in order: Natural Minor, Major, Dorian, Phrygian, Mixolydian, Melodic Minor (ascending), Harmonic Minor, Bebop Dorian, Blues, Minor Pentatonic, Hungarian Minor, Ukranian Dorian, Marva, Todi, Whole Tone, Chromatic. Note manual spelling 'Ukranian' (sic).

p32 (Root note): 'The default root note for all scales is C... Pad 9, corresponding to C, is lit a darker blue than the other pads... (Note that the keynote selection pads always show an octave from C to B.)' Pressing a different keynote pad transposes.

p32-33: 'you can change the keynote to transpose the notes, even while the Project is playing... Circuit Tracks makes an intelligent decision as to which note to play instead, which will normally be either one semitone above or below the original note. Changing scale in this way is not destructive: you can revert to the original scale and the notes will sound as they did before.'

p27: 'The two lower rows of the grid constitute the synth keyboard'; p28: 'the upper row of the synth keyboard contains notes one octave above those in the second row'; 'The synth has a total range of 10 octaves; you can access higher or lower pairs of octaves by using the down and up buttons'; p31: Chromatic yields only a one-octave keyboard. All confirmed.

p28: 'hold down Shift and press Note; Note now illuminates gold. This is called Expanded Note View, and removes the pattern display in the two upper rows... replacing it with keys for the next two higher octaves of the selected scale' (=four-octave surface). Minor imprecision in the claim: it is the NOTE BUTTON that illuminates gold, not the pads.
p60: 'Circuit Tracks has four separate drum tracks, Drum 1 to Drum 4'; p62: 'Each of the four drum tracks in Circuit Tracks can use any one of 64 pre-loaded samples.'
p62: 'four pages of 16 at a time' in Note View, 'or use Preset View... the samples are arranged as two pages of 32', opened by 'pressing the relevant track button, then Preset.'

p60 verbatim: Drum 1 = Page1 slot1 (Kick 1), Drum 2 = slot3 (Snare 1), Drum 3 = slot5 (Closed hi hat 1), Drum 4 = slot9 (Additional percussion 1). Kit layout: 1-2 kicks, 3-4 snares, 5-6 closed hats, 7-8 open hats, 9-12 (tend to be) additional percussion, 13-16 melodic. Exact.
p61: 'To change the active sample, give a different sample pad a quick tap: a longer press will play the sample but leave the previous sample assigned as the active one.'

p62-63: 'Press and hold the pad for the required sample (it turns red) and then press the step pads... they also turn red... steps with hits of the active sample illuminate bright blue, but any that have been flipped illuminate pink.' 'You can do this independently for each of the four drums.'
p63: 'Only the even-numbered Macros are active when a drum track is selected', table: Macro 2 = Pitch, 4 = Decay envelope time, 6 = Distortion, 8 = EQ. Exact.

p61: 'Expanded Drum View is the secondary view of the Note button. Open by holding Shift and pressing Note... In this View, all the grid pads except 29-32 are disabled', pads 29-32 = Playing Pads for Drums 1-4.

p90: 'Sixteen delay and eight reverb presets are provided, and you can select any one of each type. The send levels from each track... are individually adjustable for each track using the Macro controls.' p91: 'It is not possible to use different reverb presets on different tracks' (one global reverb; same for delay).

p90: 'the peach pads on Rows 1 and 2 calls up a delay preset... the cream pads on Row 3 enable reverb presets... reverb presets arranged with increasing reverberation time from Pad 17 to Pad 24, and the delay presets with increasing complexity from Pad 1 to Pad 16. In all cases, delay time is related to the BPM.'

p91 table: 1 Small Chamber, 2 Small Room 1, 3 Small Room 2, 4 Large Room, 5 Hall, 6 Large Hall, 7 Hall – long reflection, 8 Large Hall – long reflection. Exact. (Manual's table header is mislabeled 'DELAY TYPE' — a typo; it is the reverb table.)

p92 table matches all 16 in order: 1 Slapback Fast, 2 Slapback Slow, 3 32nd Triplets, 4 32nd, 5 16th Triplets, 6 16th, 7 16th Ping Pong, 8 16th Ping Pong Swung, 9 8th Triplets, 10 8th dotted Ping Pong, 11 8th, 12 8th Ping Pong, 13 8th Ping Pong Swung, 14 4th Triplets, 15 4th dotted Ping Pong Swung, 16 4th Triplets Ping Pong Wide. Exact.

p90: 'There is also a master compressor which is applied to your mix by default.' p92 (Master Compressor): 'This is enabled or disabled by the FX button in Setup View'. Not adjustable (only on/off). Page-ref nuance: v3 manual points to p102, not the claim's p106; substance correct.

p93: 'Seven Side Chain presets are available... The default state is for Side Chain to be OFF on both synths and both MIDI tracks. Side Chain View is the secondary view of the FX button. Open by holding Shift and pressing FX.'

p93-94: 'Side Chain allows the hits of the selected drum track to duck the audio level of the synths' (or external inputs). Page 1 = Synth 1/Synth 2, Page 2 = MIDI 1/MIDI 2 (external inputs). 'the first pad in each row is the OFF button... Pad 1 is illuminated bright red when the Side Chain is OFF... the seven Side Chain Presets (Pads 2 to 8 in each row).' 'Pads 5 to 8 on the top row let you select which Drum track (1-4) will be the side chain trigger.' Exact.

p94: 'Side Chain ducking will continue even when the level of the source track is reduced to zero in the Mixer View... However, if you mute the drum track selected as the key in Mixer View, side chain triggering is disabled.'

p94 (The Filter Knob): 'The entire audio output... the sum of the sounds from all six internal tracks plus the two external audio inputs... low-pass filter when you turn it anticlockwise from the centre... high-pass filter when you turn it clockwise... a detent in the centre – in this position, no filtering takes place and the LED below the knob is dimly lit white... The LED changes to pale blue when either filter type is active.'

p88: 'Circuit Tracks includes an eight-channel mixer... all the tracks play at a volume level of 100 (arbitrary units, range 0-127)... The illuminated pads on Row 1 are Mute buttons for each track.' No Solo function is documented (consistent with 'there is no Solo function').

p88: 'in Mixer View the Macros control volume level... Macro controls 3 and 4 (the MIDI tracks) control the level of external analogue audio sources connected to... Inputs 1 and 2.' p89: 'Pressing the down button converts the Macro controls to pan controls... default pan position... stereo-centre, indicated by the Macro LEDs showing white. Panning a track left turns the LED increasingly bright blue; panning to the right... bright pink.' Both level and pan are automatable (p88-89).

p18 (Rear view): 'Outputs - L/Mono and R – Circuit Tracks' main audio outputs on two ¼" TS jack sockets. Max. output level is +5.3 dBu (+/-1.5 dBu). With no plug in the R socket, the L/Mono socket carries a mono mix of L and R channels.' 'Inputs 1 and 2 – two external audio inputs... unbalanced on ¼" TS jack sockets' (line level).

p18: 'USB-C port... The port is MIDI class compliant; connect to other devices supporting MIDI via USB... NOTE – Circuit Tracks' USB port does not carry audio.' The 'must use analogue outputs to record into a DAW' is a correct inference from 'does not carry audio'.

p18: '(Headphones) ... The main outputs remain active when a plug is inserted. The headphone amplifier can drive +5 dBu into a pair of 150 ohm stereo headphones.' 'Sync – a 3.5 mm TRS jack socket supplying a clock signal of 5 V amplitude... The default rate is two pulses per quarter note.'

verification.overallNotes:
All 38 claims verified against the authoritative primary source: Novation Circuit Tracks User Guide Version 3 (circuit_tracks_user_guide_v3_en.pdf, fael-downloads-prod.focusrite.com), read directly page-by-page — not paraphrased. Page citations in the claims are accurate. Every claim is CONFIRMED. Two minor imprecisions worth flagging to the guide author, neither changing a verdict: (1) sound-17 says 'pads turn gold' in Expanded Note View — actually it is the NOTE BUTTON that illuminates gold (p28), not the pads. (2) sound-29 cites p106 for the master-compressor toggle; the v3 manual actually points to p102 (Setup View), and the compressor table header on p91 is mislabeled 'DELAY TYPE' (a Novation typo) though it lists the reverb presets.

Important sound-angle facts the claims MISSED entirely:
- FACTORY DEFAULT POLYPHONY: The factory-default Projects load Synth 1 with a MONOphonic sound and Synth 2 with a POLYphonic sound (p36 tip box) — 'so you can easily use Synth 1 for a bass line, and Synth 2 for keyboard sounds.' This qualifies sound-2: six-note polyphony is available but not the out-of-box state for Synth 1.
- SYNTH-ENGINE INTERNALS (supports sound-3, per Novation Support + MusicRadar): the Nova/MiniNova-derived engine has dual oscillators plus noise and ring modulation, multimode filters (12 and 24 dB/oct low-pass, high-pass, band-pass), three envelopes, two LFOs, and per-patch insert FX (distortion, EQ, chorus, phaser). Reviewers note it excels at digital pads/wavetable leads/dirty basses but has a digital coloration that struggles with classic analog tones.
- DRUM MIDI RECALL: Drum Patches (samples) are recalled externally via MIDI CC on Channel 10 (p62) — distinct from the synths' Program-Change recall on Channels 1/2. A sound/MIDI fact adjacent to sound-8.
- MASTER FILTER is the large knob labeled '2' on the panel (a DJ-style performance filter), one of the key performance controls (p94).
- NOTE-VIEW OVERLAP DETAIL: the highest note of the lower keyboard octave (Pad 32) is the same pitch as the lowest note of the higher octave (Pad 17) (p28).
- Sync-out rate (2 ppqn) is the DEFAULT and is adjustable in Setup View (p18); reverb/delay send automation can be deleted with Clear + Macro (p92); FX per-track send Macros show cream (reverb) or peach (delay) LEDs and adopt whichever function matches the last FX pad pressed (p91).
